text: When Ish-bosheth heard that Abner had died at Hebron, his courage failed, and all Israel was dismayed.
Saul's son had two men who were captains of raiding bands, Baanah and Rechab, sons of Rimmon of Beeroth, of the people of Benjamin — for Beeroth too is reckoned to Benjamin,
though the Beerothites had fled to Gittaim and had been sojourners there ever since.

Jonathan, Saul's son, had left a son who was crippled in his feet. He was five years old when news about Saul and Jonathan came from Jezreel; his nurse snatched him up and fled, and in her haste he fell and became lame. His name was Mephibosheth.
Rechab and Baanah, the sons of Rimmon the Beerothite, set out and came to Ish-bosheth's house about the heat of the day, while he was taking his noonday rest.
They went into the middle of the house as though to get wheat, and struck him in the stomach; then the two brothers slipped away.
They had come into the house while he lay on his bed in his bedroom; they struck him, killed him, and cut off his head, then carried it away, traveling all night by way of the Arabah,

until they brought the head of Ish-bosheth to David at Hebron, telling the king, "Here is the head of Ish-bosheth son of Saul, your enemy who sought your life — the LORD has avenged my lord the king this day on Saul and his offspring."
But David answered Rechab and his brother Baanah, sons of Rimmon the Beerothite, "As the LORD lives, who has redeemed my life out of every adversity,
when a man once told me, 'Behold, Saul is dead,' thinking he brought good news, I seized him and put him to death at Ziklag — that was the reward I gave him for his news.
How much more, then, when wicked men have killed a righteous man in his own house, on his own bed — shall I not now require his blood at your hand and remove you from the earth?"

So David commanded his young men, and they killed the two brothers, cut off their hands and feet, and hung their bodies beside the pool at Hebron; but they took Ish-bosheth's head and buried it in Abner's tomb at Hebron.
